title: taraxacum vulgare var. dens-leonis
intro: Taraxacum vulgare var. dens-leonis, also known as the Scottish dandelion, is a variety of the common dandelion plant that is native to Europe and North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and supporting liver function, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum vulgare var. dens-leonis include teas, infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
